knowledge_base_metadata.knowledge_base_scope: Rigid positional typecode parsing, valid typecode values, electrical/performance tables, enclosure dimensions, acoustic noise, and general technical specifications for iC2-Micro.

mapping_rules.mapping_logic: Resolve by matching both mains voltage class and current rating code against the relevant electrical data table.
Resolve by matching row in the applicable electrical data table. The enclosure size is table-derived, not directly encoded in the typecode except for protection rating E20.
E20 maps directly to IP20/Open Type.
F0 = C1 category with integrated EMC filter; F2 = C2 category with integrated EMC filter; F4 = C4 category without integrated EMC filter.
+ACBC = With integrated brake chopper; +ACXX = Without integrated brake chopper.
First resolve enclosure size from the electrical data row, then map enclosure size to acoustic noise using the acoustic_noise_by_enclosure table.

Resolve enclosure size from electrical data. For E20 use dimensions_ip20_open_type. For accessory-based converted versions use dimensions_ip21_type1 or dimensions_nema1 only when a conversion kit is explicitly selected outside the base typecode.

constraints.exact_rule: Base model code only defines protection rating E20: IP20/Open Type.
Built-in brake chopper is only applicable to drives in the power range of 3x380-480 V 2.2 kW and above, and 3x200-240 V 1.5 kW and above.
No BR terminals for 1x100-120 V drives, 1x200-240 V drives, 3x380-480 V 0.37-1.5 kW drives, and 3x200-240 V 0.37-0.75 kW drives.
For MA02a, only 3x200-240 V and 3x380-480 V drives have brake function. Do not connect the brake cable to MA02a 1x200-240 V drives.

fallback_rules: If a value is directly encoded, decode from typecode_values.
If a value depends on voltage class and current rating, resolve using the relevant electrical table.
If a value depends on enclosure size, resolve enclosure size first, then use enclosure-based tables.
If documents conflict, return CONFLICT or the unresolved_value_marker and log the conflict.
If a value is missing from both documents, return NOT FOUND IN THE UPLOADED DOCUMENTS.
